name: Mint (Intuit)
notes: Mint has been transitioning users to Credit Karma. Cancel instructions may vary depending on migration status.

name: Amazon Prime Video
cancel_steps: Go to amazon.com and sign in. Note: Canceling Prime Video alone is not straightforward — Amazon bundles video with the full Prime membership. To cancel video-only, you need to cancel Prime entirely or navigate to a specific page.
Go to amazon.com/mc/pipelines/prime to access your Prime membership settings.
Click 'Manage membership', then 'End membership'.

Navigate through multiple confirmation screens — Amazon presents several offers and warnings before completing cancellation. You may see 3-4 screens with alternative offers (pause, discounts, reminders of benefits) before the final confirmation button.
Click 'Cancel membership' to confirm.
known_friction: Cancellation screens rotate between having the 'yes cancel' and 'no keep my subscription' buttons swap their visual prominence (green vs gray), making it hard to know which button cancels vs keeps.; Amazon's cancel flow is notoriously long and arduous, with multiple screens presenting benefits, discounts, and pause options before confirming. The FTC sued Amazon over this practice in 2023.; Amazon Prime Video is bundled with Prime shipping, making it unclear how to cancel video-only access vs. th

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

free_alternatives.name: Pluto TV
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

free_alternatives.name: Freevee (Amazon)
free_alternatives.note: Amazon's free ad-supported tier, accessible without Prime

notes: The FTC filed a complaint against Amazon in 2023 alleging that their cancellation process used 'dark patterns' including 'Iliad flow' — a maze of screens designed to wear users down. A class action settlement covers US users who subscribed between June 23, 2019 and June 23, 2025 via a 'challenged enrollment flow.' Amazon updated some flows post-FTC action, but user reports still cite significant friction as of early 2026.

name: Apple Music
cancel_steps: Go to music.apple.com and sign in with your Apple ID. On iPhone/iPad: Settings > [Your Name] > Subscriptions > Apple Music > Cancel Subscription.
Click the My Account icon, then select 'Settings'.
Scroll to 'Subscriptions' and click 'Manage'.
Select Apple Music and click 'Cancel Subscription'. Confirm when prompted. Music access ends at the end of the billing period. Downloaded songs become unplayable after cancellation.
known_friction: Retention tactics: None widely reported. Apple's subscription system is consistent and low-friction.

free_alternatives.name: Spotify (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free with ads and shuffle-only mobile

free_alternatives.name: YouTube Music (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free with ads

free_alternatives.name: SoundCloud (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free with ads, strong indie/underground catalog

notes: Apple Music cancellation uses the same flow as all Apple subscriptions. Note that any music downloaded for offline listening will become unplayable after cancellation — unlike Spotify, there is no free tier to fall back to.

name: Apple TV+
cancel_steps: Go to tv.apple.com and sign in with your Apple ID. You can also cancel through iPhone/iPad: Settings > [Your Name] > Subscriptions > Apple TV+ > Cancel Subscription.
Click your account icon, then select 'Settings'.
Scroll to 'Subscriptions' and click 'Manage'.
Select Apple TV+ and click 'Cancel Subscription'. Confirm when prompted. Access continues through end of billing period.
known_friction: Retention tactics: None widely reported. Apple's subscription management is considered clean and low-friction.

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

free_alternatives.name: Pluto TV
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

notes: Apple TV+ cancellation is handled through Apple's standard subscription management system, which is consistent across all Apple services. No significant dark patterns reported. Often bundled with new Apple device purchases (3-month free trial).

name: Audible
cancel_steps: Go to audible.com and sign in. Cancellation is NOT available through the Audible mobile app — desktop/web only.
Click your profile name in the top right, then select 'Account details'.

Under 'Membership details', click 'Cancel membership'. If you don't see this option, check which region's site you're on. UK users must use audible.co.uk, US users must use audible.com. Being on the wrong regional site is a known trap.
Navigate through Audible's retention flow — you will be offered credits, pauses, and discounted plans before the final cancel button appears.
Select a cancellation reason and click 'Cancel membership' to confirm. Any unused credits/coins may be lost. Keep in mind books already purchased remain in your library forever.
known_friction: Cancellation is not available through the Audible mobile app — users must use a desktop browser, creating friction for mobile-first users.; US users accessing audible.co.uk (or vice versa) get stuck in a loop where the cancel option never appears. This is a known dark pattern reported by users as recently as January 2026.; A UX designer commenting in 2024 noted that cancellation appears 'literally impossible on mobile' and that dark patterns are used to obscure the path even for people who know

free_alternatives.name: Libby
free_alternatives.note: Free audiobooks and ebooks through your public library

free_alternatives.name: Overdrive
free_alternatives.note: Free library-based audiobook lending

free_alternatives.name: Spotify (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Growing audiobook catalog; free tier has limited audiobook hours

notes: Audible is one of the highest-friction streaming cancellations. Key gotchas: (1) mobile app can't cancel, (2) regional site mismatch causes endless loops, (3) multi-screen retention flow. Unused credits are lost on cancellation. Books already purchased remain in your library indefinitely even after cancellation. The FTC's Click-to-Cancel Rule (July 2025) may pressure Audible to simplify this flow.

name: Disney+
cancel_steps: Go to disneyplus.com and sign in.
Click your profile icon, then select 'Account'.
Under your subscription, click 'Cancel Subscription'.
Confirm the cancellation when prompted. Access continues through the end of the billing period.
known_friction: Users may be shown the Disney Bundle (Disney+/Hulu/ESPN+) as a retention offer before cancellation is confirmed.; Users who subscribed via Apple App Store, Google Play, Amazon, or Roku cannot cancel on the Disney+ website — must cancel through the original billing platform. | Retention tactics: Offers bundle downgrade instead of full cancellation.; Shows upcoming Marvel/Star Wars content to trigger FOMO.

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

free_alternatives.name: YouTube (free)
free_alternatives.note: Free content including many Disney classics uploaded officially

notes: If subscribed through a third-party (Apple, Google, Amazon, Roku, or via a cable provider), cancellation must happen on that platform — Disney+ website will not show a cancel option. Price increases in 2024-2025 drove significant subscriber churn.

name: HBO Max
cancel_steps: Go to max.com and sign in. The service rebranded from HBO Max to 'Max' in 2023. Cancellation steps are the same.
Click your profile icon, then select 'Settings'.
Select 'Subscription' or 'Billing'.
Click 'Cancel Plan' and follow the prompts. If you subscribed through a TV provider, cable company, or third-party app store, you must cancel through that provider.
known_friction: Max has one of the most complex billing ecosystems of any streaming service — subscribers may come from Amazon, Apple, Roku, YouTube TV, AT&T, Hulu add-on, or direct billing. Cancellation path varies significantly by source, and the website doesn't always clearly identify which path applies to you.; Late 2025 price increases to $10.99/$18.49/$22.99 across tiers, combined with complex cancellation paths, led to significant user confusion. | Retention tactics: Offers to pause subscription before c

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

free_alternatives.name: Pluto TV
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

notes: The rebrand from HBO Max to Max in 2023 added confusion. Cancellation difficulty depends heavily on billing source — direct billing is straightforward; third-party billing requires going to that platform. If you subscribed via a cable bundle (AT&T, Spectrum, etc.), you may need to call your provider.

name: Hulu
cancel_steps: Go to hulu.com and sign in.
Click your profile name in the top right, then select 'Account'.
Under 'Your Subscription', click 'Cancel'.
Follow the prompts to confirm cancellation. If you subscribed through a third party (Spotify, Disney Bundle, etc.), you must cancel through that platform instead.
known_friction: Users who subscribed through bundle deals or third parties cannot cancel on Hulu's website and must navigate to a different platform, creating friction. | Retention tactics: Offers a pause option before confirming cancellation.; May present a discounted plan offer before confirming.

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

free_alternatives.name: Pluto TV
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

notes: Cancellation method depends heavily on how you subscribed. Direct subscribers can cancel at hulu.com. Those on the Disney Bundle, or who subscribed through Apple, Amazon, or Roku must cancel through those platforms. Deleting the app does NOT cancel the subscription.

name: Kindle Unlimited
cancel_steps: Go to amazon.com/ku/manage and sign in. Direct URL: amazon.com/kindle-dbs/hz/subscribe/ku?ref=ku_mng_stng
Click 'Cancel Kindle Unlimited Membership'.

A confirmation screen will show your membership end date — click 'Cancel membership' to confirm. You can continue reading borrowed books until the end of the billing period. After that, borrowed titles are removed from your device.
known_friction: Amazon requires two separate confirmation clicks before completing cancellation. | Retention tactics: Shows how many books you've read to create sunk-cost attachment.

free_alternatives.name: Libby
free_alternatives.note: Free ebooks and audiobooks through your public library

free_alternatives.name: Project Gutenberg
free_alternatives.note: Free public domain ebooks

free_alternatives.name: Amazon Prime Reading
free_alternatives.note: Limited free ebook selection included with Amazon Prime

notes: Kindle Unlimited is one of the simpler Amazon cancellations — unlike Prime, it has its own dedicated management page. The double-confirmation is mildly annoying but not adversarial. Borrowed books are removed after the billing period ends; purchased Kindle books are unaffected.

name: Medium
cancel_steps: Go to medium.com and sign in.
Click your profile picture, then select 'Settings'.
Click 'Manage Subscription'.
Click 'Cancel membership' and confirm. If you subscribed via the iOS app, you must cancel through Apple's subscription management. If via Google Play, cancel through Google Play.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May ask for feedback on why you're canceling before confirming.

free_alternatives.name: Medium (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Limited free articles per month without a subscription

free_alternatives.name: Substack
free_alternatives.note: Free newsletter/blog reading; individual writers may charge

free_alternatives.name: Dev.to
free_alternatives.note: Free tech/dev focused writing platform

notes: Medium is one of the lowest-friction subscription cancels. No dark patterns, no retention maze. Third-party billing (App Store, Google Play) requires canceling through those platforms. The free tier still allows limited article reading.

name: Netflix
cancel_steps: Go to netflix.com and sign in.
Click your profile icon in the top right, then select 'Account'.
Under 'Membership & Billing', click 'Cancel Membership'.
Click 'Finish Cancellation' to confirm. You can keep using Netflix until the end of your current billing period.
known_friction: Netflix warns that your profile watch history and recommendations will be lost, creating emotional friction before confirming cancellation.; Shows upcoming content and what you'll lose access to before confirming, designed to trigger regret. | Retention tactics: Offers to pause membership instead of canceling.; Shows 'What you'll lose' screen highlighting upcoming releases.

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming with large catalog

free_alternatives.name: Pluto TV
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported live and on-demand streaming

free_alternatives.name: Peacock (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Limited free tier available

notes: Netflix is considered one of the easier streaming services to cancel — the process is fully online and straightforward. The main friction is emotional (loss aversion screens), not procedural. Direct cancel URL works without navigating through account settings.

name: Paramount+
cancel_steps: Go to paramountplus.com and sign in.
Click your profile icon, then select 'Account'.
Under 'Subscription', choose 'Paramount+', then select 'Cancel Subscription'.
Follow the prompts to confirm. Access continues through the end of the billing period. If subscribed through Amazon, Apple, Roku, or another platform, cancel there instead.
known_friction: Subscribers through Amazon Channels, Apple, Roku, or cable providers must cancel through the originating platform. Paramount+ website won't show a cancel option for these subscribers. | Retention tactics: May offer a pause or discounted rate before confirming cancellation.

free_alternatives.name: Pluto TV
free_alternatives.note: Owned by Paramount; free ad-supported tier with some Paramount content

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

notes: Generally considered a low-friction cancel. Third-party billing is the main gotcha. Pluto TV (free, also owned by Paramount) is the natural free alternative for users who want to keep some access to Paramount content.

name: Peacock
cancel_steps: Go to peacocktv.com and sign in.
Click your profile icon, then select 'Account'.
Under 'Plan & Payment', click 'Change Plan'.
Select 'Cancel Plan' and confirm. Some users report needing to contact customer support if online cancellation fails. Access continues until end of billing period.
known_friction: The cancel option is nested under 'Change Plan' rather than having its own obvious cancel button, making it less discoverable. | Retention tactics: May offer a discounted plan before confirming cancellation.

free_alternatives.name: Peacock (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Peacock has a limited free ad-supported tier

free_alternatives.name: Tubi
free_alternatives.note: Free ad-supported streaming

notes: Peacock has a free tier, so users may be able to downgrade rather than fully cancel. Some NBC content is available free. If subscribed via Apple, Amazon, or Roku, cancel through that platform.

name: Spotify
cancel_steps: Go to spotify.com/account and sign in.
Click 'Manage your plan' or go directly to spotify.com/account/subscription.
Click 'Change or cancel' then select 'Cancel Premium'.
Navigate through the retention screens — Spotify presents emotional appeals and FOMO content before the final confirmation.
Click 'Yes, cancel' to confirm. You revert to the free tier (with ads) rather than losing access entirely.
known_friction: Despite only requiring 5 clicks, Spotify uses guilt-trip messaging, 'what you'll miss' screens, and FOMO-based content previews during the cancellation flow. Under the FTC's Click-to-Cancel Rule (effective July 2025), some of these tactics may be non-compliant.; Some screens use emotionally loaded button labels (e.g., 'Keep my Premium' prominently styled vs. the cancel option). | Retention tactics: Offers a discounted rate (often 1-3 months at reduced price) before confirming cancellation.; Show

free_alternatives.name: Spotify (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Full catalog access with ads and shuffle-only on mobile

free_alternatives.name: YouTube Music (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Free with ads

free_alternatives.name: Amazon Music (free tier)
free_alternatives.note: Limited free tier included with Prime

notes: Spotify doesn't remove access when you cancel Premium — you drop to the free ad-supported tier. This reduces the urgency of the cancellation decision. The emotional manipulation during the flow is well-documented but the actual process is not procedurally difficult.

name: YouTube Premium
cancel_steps: Go to youtube.com/paid_memberships and sign in. On mobile: tap your profile picture > Paid memberships.
Select YouTube Premium.
Click 'Manage' then 'Cancel membership'. If you subscribed via Google Play, Apple, or another platform, you must cancel through that platform instead.
Confirm cancellation. Access continues through the end of the billing period. Ads return immediately after cancellation date.
known_friction: Retention tactics: May offer a pause option before confirming cancellation.

free_alternatives.name: YouTube (free)
free_alternatives.note: Full YouTube access with ads — same content, no offline or background play

free_alternatives.name: uBlock Origin (browser extension)
free_alternatives.note: Free ad blocker for desktop browsers

notes: YouTube Premium is one of the more straightforward cancels. The free tier of YouTube retains all content access — you lose ad-free viewing, background play, and offline downloads.
